data_formats: Widely used file formats (Spreadsheet, JSON, GeoJSON, SHP, GPX, KML, XML, TIFF, MP3, MP4, etc.)
name: EAGLE concept
description:
The EAGLE concept dataset, developed within the Copernicus Land Monitoring Service, is a semantic data model designed to describe land in a flexible and harmonised way. Rather than relying on a single predefined class for each spatial unit (e.g., “forest” or “urban”), EAGLE represents land through a structured combination of attributes. It separates information into three complementary components: Land Cover Components (biophysical elements such as vegetation or water), Land Use Attributes (the socio-economic function, such as agriculture or residential use), and Land Characteristics (additional contextual properties like management practices or environmental conditions).

This multi-dimensional approach enables a more detailed and adaptable representation of land, supporting complex landscapes and mixed-use areas without forcing them into rigid categories. The EAGLE framework also facilitates interoperability between different land monitoring datasets by acting as a common semantic layer, allowing data from diverse classification systems to be compared, translated, and integrated. As such, it provides a robust foundation for European land monitoring initiatives, including products derived from Copernicus, and supports more consistent analysis, reporting, and policy applications across regions and time.
data_ownership: Owned and maintained by the European Environment Agency (EEA), in collaboration with the Copernicus Land Monitoring Service under the Copernicus programme.
data_contact: European Environment Information and Observation Network (Eionet) through info@eea.europa.eu
access_barrier: No, direct download/access through a web link
documentation_list:
Arnold, S., Smith, G., Strand, G.-H., Hazeu, G., Bock, M., Kosztra, B., Perger, C., Banko, G., Soukup, T., Sanz, N. V., Kleeschulte, S., Hernández, J. D., & Mancosu, E. (2025). Recent Advances in the EAGLE Concept—Monitoring the Earth’s Surface Based on a New Land Characterisation Approach. Land, 14(8), 1525. https://doi.org/10.3390/land14081525

Arnold, Stephan & Kosztra, Barbara & Banko, Gebhard & Smith, Geoff & Hazeu, G.W. & Bock, Michael & Valcarcel, Nuria. (2013). The EAGLE concept – A vision of a future European Land Monitoring Framework. EARSeL symposium proceedings "Towards Horizon 2020".
